---
title: "hgp_predict"
generated: true
description: "Posterior population (or per-group) curves at `x_new`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.hgp`](../nitrix-stats-hgp.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:hgp_predict -->
```python
hgp_predict(result: HGPResult, x_new: Float[Array, ' g'], *, levels: Optional[Int[Array, ' L']] = None, parametric: Optional[Float[Array, 'g q']] = None) -> Tuple[Float[Array, '...'], Float[Array, '...']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:hgp_predict -->
Posterior population (or per-group) curves at `x_new`.

With `levels=None` (default) returns the **population** smooth
`(mean, std)` -- each `(V, g)` -- the fixed effects plus `Phi(x_new)
beta_pop`.  With `levels` a sequence of group indices, returns the
**group** curves (population + that group's deviation) shaped
`(V, len(levels), g)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:hgp_predict -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `HGPResult` | — | — | An `HGPResult` from `hgp_fit`. |
| `x_new` | `Float` | `g` | — | `(g,)` covariate grid. |
| `levels` | `Optional[Int[Array, ' L']]` | — | `None` | Optional group indices to render; `None` gives the population curve. |
| `parametric` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'g q']]` | — | `None` | `(g, q)` parametric covariates (required if the fit used them). |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, '...'], Float[Array, '...']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:hgp_predict -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `g` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
